commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Surely there was not held such a passover from the days of the judges that judged Israel, nor in all the days of the kings of Israel, nor of the kings of Judah; No such, in all respects. (Haydock) The number of paschal lambs was certainly greater when all Israel was assembled; but the other victims presented by the king and his officers during the octave is here noticed, (2 Paralipomenon xxxv. ; Menochius) as they are also styled the Phase; (Haydock) and this explains John xviii. (Tirinus) Neither ought we to push these expressions too far, as they only mean, that this solemnity was very great.
